2604.11399.title: Reasoning Resides in Layers: Restoring Temporal Reasoning in Video-Language Models with Layer-Selective Merging
2604.11399.paper_summary: Multimodal adaptation equips large language models (LLMs) with perceptual capabilities, but often weakens the reasoning ability inherited from language-only pretraining. This trade-off is especially pronounced in video-language models (VLMs), where visual alignment can impair temporal reasoning (TR) over sequential events. We propose MERIT, a training-free, task-driven model merging framework for restoring TR in VLMs. MERIT searches over layer-wise self-attention merging recipes between a VLM and its paired text-only backbone using an objective that improves TR while penalizing degradation in temporal perception (TP). Across three representative VLMs and multiple challenging video benchmarks, MERIT consistently improves TR, preserves or improves TP, and generalizes beyond the search set to four distinct benchmarks. It also outperforms uniform full-model merging and random layer selection, showing that effective recovery depends on selecting the right layers. Interventional masking and frame-level attribution further show that the selected layers are disproportionately important for reasoning and shift model decisions toward temporally and causally relevant evidence. These results show that targeted, perception-aware model merging can effectively restore TR in VLMs without retraining.
2604.11399.paper_summary_zh: 多模態適應賦予大型語言模型（LLMs）感知能力，但通常會削弱從僅語言預訓練中繼承的推理能力。這種權衡在視頻語言模型（VLMs）中尤其明顯，視覺對齊可能會損害對連續事件的時間推理（TR）。我們提出了MERIT，一種無需訓練的、以任務為驅動的模型合併框架，用於恢復VLM中的TR。MERIT在VLM及其配對的僅文本骨幹之間搜索層級自注意力合併方案，使用一個目標來改善TR，同時懲罰時間感知（TP）的退化。在三個代表性的VLM和多個具有挑戰性的視頻基準上，MERIT始終改善TR，保持或改善TP，並超越搜索集在四個不同的基準上進行泛化。它還優於均勻的全模型合併和隨機層選擇，顯示有效的恢復依賴於選擇正確的層。干預性遮罩和幀級歸因進一步顯示所選層對推理的重要性不成比例，並將模型決策轉向時間上和因果上相關的證據。這些結果顯示，針對性的、感知意識的模型合併可以有效地在VLM中恢復TR，而無需重新訓練。
2604.11399.author: Zihang Fu et.al.
2604.11399.authors: Zihang Fu, Haonan Wang, Jian Kang, Kenji Kawaguchi, Jiaying Wu
